text.title: Growth Conversion Optimization Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Growth Conversion Optimization` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: A/B winners MUST hit ≥ 95% statistical significance (p < 0.05). 'Looks like it's working' calls are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Sub-95% calls cause false-positive winners; the floor is the industry default for CRO decision-making.

rule.statement: Each variant MUST accumulate ≥ 100 conversions before the test can be called. Below the floor, the experiment is statistically invalid.
rule.rationale: Below 100 conv / variant the confidence interval is too wide; the floor protects against false declarations.

rule.statement: Each test MUST run ≥ 14 days (covering 2 weekly cycles) before being called. Short-window calls are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Tests called inside one week miss weekday/weekend variance; 14 days is the minimum for cyclical baselining.

rule.statement: Each hypothesis MUST cite ≥ 1 research input (session recording / heatmap / interview / survey / analytics segment). Vibes hypotheses are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vibes hypotheses fail at 70%+; research-grounded hypotheses succeed at 30-50% — significant lift over baseline.

rule.statement: Each completed test (win or loss) MUST be recorded in a learnings library with hypothesis, variant, sample, result, lesson. Lost-only tests are still recorded.
rule.rationale: Without a library, the same losing test is run by next year's team; the library compounds learning across cycles.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
